title: Sun Guard
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Thyrsus Sun Guard}}
{{Youmay|the organization|a type of hat known as a [[sunguard]]}}
{{Youmay|the Sun Guard|the [[Solar Guard]]}}
{{Update|[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]}}

|image=[[File:SunGuard-GAW.jpg]]

|subgroup=

|affiliation=*Thyrsus<ref name="Author's Cut" />
*[[Order of the Sith Lords]]<ref name="END">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>
*[[Emperor's Shadow Guard|Imperial Shadow Guard]]<ref name="GAW" />
}}
{{Quote|[Royal Guard] armor is derived from a pair of similar designs, the uniforms of the Death Watch of the Mandalore system and the Sun Guards of the Thyrsus system. That the armor resembles those of units with such ferocious reputations is no accident…|[[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]]|<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
The '''Sun Guard''' was a [[Thyrsian]] [[mercenary]] company and one of the most feared in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], centered on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]] in the [[Thyrsus system/Legends|Thyrsus system]]. Formed from the rebellious [[Thyrsian special forces]] sometime after [[1154 BBY]], the Sun Guard fought the [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] of the [[Echani Command]] in numerous skirmishes before the [[Pact of Almera]] in [[899 BBY]] confirmed Thyrsus's independence. Earning fame as a mercenary unit, the Sun Guard became rivals of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warriors, though the group was ultimately subverted by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] under the influence of the [[Order of the Sith Lords]]. Many Sun Guards served the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] directly, led by their [[Supreme Sun Guardian]] [[Thull Wulain]], and other Sun Guards were considered for the position of [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. The [[2nd Regiment]] of the Sun Guard fought in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], but were crushed by Republic [[General/Legends|general]] [[Solomahal/Legends|Solomahal]]. When Sidious needed the Sun Guard no longer, his apprentice [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] ordered his own minion [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] to eliminate them. Some of the few surviving Sun Guards went on to join Supreme Chancellor Palpatine's [[Red Guard]], [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]], and for those few [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] individuals, the [[Emperor's Shadow Guard|Shadow Guard]]. A new generation of Sun Guards arose during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] almost fifty years later, defending Thyrsus against the extragalactic [[Yuuzhan Vong]] and seeking to restore their group's traditions and reputations.

Though most were not [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive, the Sun Guard were adept melee fighters, these Echani men and women equipped with a variety of weapons built into their recognizable [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. Greatly prizing [[Unarmed combat/Legends|hand-to-hand combat]], the Sun Guard were skilled in personal [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] fighting, urban warfare, and a variety of other techniques; they were unmatched by any opponents but the Mandalorians. The Sun Guards' chief weapon was the [[Pike/Legends|pike]], a two-handed bladed instrument that could be built in a number of variations for specific tasks. Sun Guard armor proved to be an inspiration for many other armor designs in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]: the uniforms of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guard]]; the specialized [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] armors designed by [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Nashiak Llalik]]; the outfit of [[Carnor Jax]], son of a Sun Guard; and the Sun Guard replica armor of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Moxin Tark]].

==Organization and philosophy==
Initially, the Sun Guard served as a special-forces unit of the [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]] military, incorporated into its rank structure. In opposition to their [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] forebears, the [[Thyrsian]]s were [[Gender/Legends|male]]-dominated, with an emphasis on [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and bladed combat. A strongly religious group in its infancy, the Sun Guard drew spiritual significance from the [[Star/Legends|sun]]. As they spread into [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from Thyrsus, the unit used its near-unmatched skills in combat to its advantage, reorganizing itself into a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] group along the lines of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] and forgoing religion as its primary motivation. The influence of religion returned to the forefront when the Sun Guard became under the spell of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], though this new allegiance did not prevent them from continuing their mercenary ways and demanding payment for their services.<ref name="GAW" /> Fierce warriors, the Sun Guard were a devoted and fanatical group,<ref name="END" /> with a reputation for ferocity that made even the appearance of their uniforms an object of fear.<ref name="ISB" /> They charged high prices for their services, which included sabotage, kidnapping, rescue, warfare, and many other kinds of jobs; the one thing Sun Guard missions had in common was that others had tried to accomplish them and failed.<ref name="GAW" />

As a military unit, the Guard was organized into [[Regiment/Legends|regiments]],<ref name="RaT">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040325adv|text=Rough and Tundra|int=Rough and Tundra}}</ref> [[Legion/Legends|legions]], and other levels of hierarchy; they often operated in teams of four. The lowest rank, taken by initiates into the group, was [[Stellar Legionnaire]]. They served a provisional term of one [[Standard year/Legends|year]], during which [[Time/Legends|time]] they could be expelled at any point for breaches of Sun Guard etiquette, such as cowardice or undisciplined drunkenness. Legionnaires who passed were promoted to [[Stellar Tribune]] and made responsible for a legion of the Sun Guard, consisting of twenty to forty [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref name="GAW" /> [[Officer/Legends|Officers]] were rarer among the Sun Guard, as only particularly heroic Tribunes could be granted admission to the officer corps; officer ranks ranged from [[Twisuns Legate]], who commanded two to four legions, [[Twisuns Praetor]], [[Thychani Commander]], [[Thychani Dictator]], and finally [[Supreme Sun Guardian]], which was the supreme commander of the entire Sun Guard.<ref name="Author's Cut" /> Promotion was obtained through military heroism, while cowardice was cause for expulsion at any rank.<ref name="GAW" />

==History==
===Formation and early history===
[[File:SunGuard.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A member of the Sun Guard.]]
{{Quote|You'll never get outta here alive.|[[Thug leader|Sun Guard]] to [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]] and [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]|<ref name="TPMvg">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]]</ref>}}
The Sun Guard originated from the [[Thyrsian special forces|special forces]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]], which was inhabited by the [[near-Human]] [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and was part of the [[Echani Command]], a confederation of six worlds known as the [[Six Sisters]] that were ruled by a [[Gender/Legends|female]] council. While the Thyrsians shared the Echani's skill at reading the smallest cues of body language, their culture differed from typical Echani culture in a number of ways: Thyrsus was male-dominated, Thyrsians had dark [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and skin, they were spiritually focused on the [[Star/Legends|sun]] instead of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]], and they favored bladed weapons and heavy [[Armor/Legends|armor]] instead of hand-to-hand combat. Thyrsus seceded from the Echani Command along with the planet [[Begali]] in the [[Begali Uprising]] of [[1154 BBY]], but though Begali renewed its allegiance to the planet [[Eshan/Legends|Eshan]] less than a century later, Thyrsus remained fiercely independent,<ref name="Author's Cut" /> and their military units took on a sigil of Thyrsus' red suns.<ref name="GAW" /> The planet's special forces became known as the Sun Guard as they evolved over the centuries, and they fought numerous battles with the Echani before the [[Pact of Almera]] confirmed Thyrsus's independence in [[899 BBY]].<ref name="Author's Cut" />

As centuries of peace among the Six Sisters passed, the Sun Guard became more independent, largely abandoning Thyrsus to adopt a semi-nomadic way of life as a famed mercenary unit. Many Sun Guards served as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and gladiators, and also served in various conflicts throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The Sun Guard soon became rivals of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior culture,<ref name="Author's Cut" /> whose expansion alarmed the Sun Guard, and members of both groups often fought in small duels to the death rather than military engagements. The Sun Guard developed new tactics to counter the Mandalorians' reliance on [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]] in air combat, which the Sun Guards held in contempt.<ref name="GAW" /> The Sun Guard's rivalry with the Mandalorians lasted for centuries, reaching its peak at the [[Battle of Sintheti]] in [[402 BBY/Legends|402 BBY]]: both groups were hired by rival claimants to the [[Horned Throne]], and they dueled and fought each other for more than three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] amid the [[Sintheti|planet's]] crags and catacombs.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

The Sun Guard began with a strong focus on religion, and though they eventually turned to a mercenary outlook upon realizing that warfare led only to poverty, religion retained its influence on them. Over a century before the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the culture of Thyrsus developed a fixation on matters of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], especially a prophecy of a galactic savior known as "the [[Son of Suns/Legends|son of suns]]." The [[Order of the Sith Lords]], in hiding during this time, played up the Thyrsian belief that they were the ones spoken of in the prophecy, encouraging the spread of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] interpretations of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] belief. The Sun Guards were transformed into a Sith cult.<ref name="GAW" />

===In the service of Darth Plagueis===
[[File:Darth_Plagueis.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Sith Lord Darth Plagueis commanded a unit of Sun Guards during his lifetime.]]
{{Quote|Contact the Sun Guard. Have them ready a ship and prepare yourself to accompany them.|Darth Plagueis, ordering the droid 11-4D to send the Sun Guard to cover up the murder of the family of Plagueis' associate Palpatine|<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />}}
During the last century of the Republic, the [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]] employed a [[Company/Legends|company]] of [[Color/Legends|silver]]-armored Echani Sun Guards to provide security at his residence on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Sojourn (moon)|Sojourn]], replacing the [[Iotran/Legends|Iotran]] [[Iotran Brandsmen|Brandsmen]] that had previously defended the world. A contingent of Echani also provided protection for members of the [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]]-based company [[Damask Holdings/Legends|Damask Holdings]], a firm that Plagueis led in his public persona, "Hego Damask." A powerful [[Banker/Legends|financier]], Damask brokered a deal with [[Foreman/Legends|Foreman]] [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] of the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] to make the planet [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]] available to the Sun Guard as a training ground.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

In [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]], several Sun Guards accompanied Damask's deputy [[Larsh Hill]] and other Damask Holdings members to [[High Port Space Center]] on Muunilinst, to rendezvous with Damask—who had just arrived from an off-world trip—at the port's [[High Port Immigration|customs control section]]. A short time later, Damask staged a [[Gathering (Sojourn)|Gathering]], an annual meeting of influential beings on Sojourn, and his Sun Guards oversaw the event. During the conclave, Plagueis ordered the commander of the Sun Guard contingent to strand four [[Gossam/Legends|Gossam]] senior managers of the firm [[Subtext Mining]] on a remote world in the [[Tingel Arm/Legends|Tingel Arm]], after the managers' actions earned Plagueis' ire. The Guards also detected a perimeter breach when the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Darth Venamis]], a rival Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] who sought to challenge Plagueis, arrived on Sojourn during the Gathering. Plagueis [[Contest on Sojourn|defeated]] Venamis in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]], and ordered the Sun Guard to locate Venamis' [[Starship/Legends|ship]], then transport the vessel and the incapacitated Bith to [[Aborah|Plagueis' residence]] on Muunilinst.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

Two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, in [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]], Plagueis dispatched his Sun Guards to recover the stranded Gossams from the Tingel Arm and execute them, then leave their bodies outside Subtext Mining's headquarters on the world [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], to act as a warning to the firm. Later that year, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], an associate of Damask, murdered [[House Palpatine/Legends|his family]] aboard [[Palpatine family starship|their starship]] while traveling along the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]. In desperation, Palpatine contacted Damask for help, and the Muun dispatched some Sun Guards and the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[11-4D/Legends|11-4D]] to take control of Palpatine's ship and cover up the young man's crime. In the aftermath of the massacre, Palpatine became Plagueis' [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], under the name "Darth Sidious."<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

Around that time, the Sith engineered the rise of a Sun Guard named [[Thull Wulain]] to the position of Supreme Sun Guardian, and Wulain swore fealty to Darth Sidious upon achieving the position.<ref name="Author's Cut" /> The Sun Guard went on the provide security at another Gathering that Damask held on Sojourn in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]]. Sometime afterward, Palpatine was captured by [[Santhe Security]], allies of Plagueis' rival [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Pax Teem]], and he was taken to a deserted factory in [[The Works/Legends|The Works]] region of the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. At Plagueis' behest, a team of Sun Guards assaulted the facility and rescued Palpatine, then defeated the Santhe Security agents. The Guardsmen traced the source of a signal from Teem to the [[Panoply Orbital Facility]] above Coruscant, and Plagueis sent the entire team of Sun Guards, bar two, to attack the [[Space station/Legends|station]]. With the two reserve Sun Guards as his escort, Plagueis made his way to the [[Lodge of the Canted Circle|lodge]] of the [[Order of the Canted Circle]], an elite social order that Larsh Hill was to be initiated into. During Hill's inauguration ceremony, a detachment of [[Maladian]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] hired by Teem [[Attack on the Lodge of the Canted Circle|attacked]] the lodge, in an attempt on Damask's life. The two Sun Guards fell while defending their employer, but not before killing several of the Maladians, and Plagueis ultimately survived the raid.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

Two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the failed assassination attempt, Plagueis summoned Palpatine to Muunilinst, and two Sun Guards escorted the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] to Plagueis' residence in an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]]. Damask became increasingly reclusive after the attack, and over the following years he retreated to Sojourn, where he continued to retain a small company of Sun Guards. The Guards' duties were limited to escorting Damask's few visitors and maintaining the moon's ground-based [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], and Damask rarely communicated with the Guardsmen, only relaying orders to them through 11-4D. Plagueis left Palpatine, who was now a senator in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], to progress on his own, and the Sun Guards informed the Human of his master's obsessive and erratic behavior.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|King]] [[Ars Veruna/Legends|Ars Veruna]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] staged a [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear]] [[Bombing of Sojourn|attack]] on Sojourn, in an attempt to eliminate Damask. The Sun Guards stationed on the moon perished during the bombardment, but Plagueis survived, and he considered the Guards to have deserved their fate, because they had failed to prevent the attack. The Muun contacted the Sun Guards on Thyrsus to hire some replacement Guardsmen and at least four Sun Guards protected Damask in his Coruscant [[Darth Plagueis' apartment/Legends|penthouse]], his new permanent residence.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

===Agents of Lord Sidious===
Sidious used his influence as a Sith to consolidate the Sun Guard in the [[Thyrsus system/Legends|Thyrsus system]] under his command sometime before [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Sidious assigned a number of them to guard his stronghold on Coruscant, where they would eliminate any underdweller who approached too closely. The Sun Guard proved essential in Sidious' plans regarding the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], as he used them to assassinate reluctant businessmen, as well as several pivotal senators<ref name="GAW" /><ref name="END" /> before his planned election to the position of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] as Palpatine of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> Sidious' apprentice [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] used the Sun Guard as opponents in his practice duels.<ref name="GAW" /> Shortly after the invasion of Naboo, a [[Coruscant mercenary|group of mercenaries]] led by a [[Thug leader|Sun Guard]] kidnapped [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and imprisoned her in the [[Restricted Area]] of the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant Underworld]]. The [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of her guard, [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]], rescued the Queen and killed the Sun Guard.<ref name="TPMvg" /> Soon afterward, Palpatine's plans came to fruition, and he was nominated as a candidate to become the new Supreme Chancellor of the Republic. Plagueis gave his Sun Guards the night off on the day of Palpatine's [[Supreme Chancellor election, 32 BBY/Legends|election]] and, in their absence, Palpatine murdered his master and became the new reigning Sith Lord.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

[[File:SunGuard2.jpg|thumb|right|175px|A Sun Guard in Darth Sidious' employ.]]
During the Sith Lords' search for a [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] for the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Sun Guards from the ranks of the [[Thychani Commander]]s and Thychani Dictators were among the candidates considered by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]].<ref name="Author's Cut" /> In [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]], the [[Holocron of King Adas|holocron]] of the ancient [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[King/Legends|king]] [[Adas]] was recovered by the student [[Norval]],<ref name="EGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> and Sidious dispatched a [[Carnor Jax's father|Sun Guard]] to retrieve it.<ref name="END" /> The Guard intercepted Norval's vessel over the world of [[Ploo II/Legends|Ploo II]]. Jedi [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] arrived in pursuit; the Guard opened fire on them and, when Kenobi left for Norval's vessel, turned his weapons on that ship. Though Norval was confident the Sun Guard would save him, the Jedi retrieved the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], and the Guard destroyed Norval when it became apparent he no longer had the prize, in a failed attempt to prevent the Jedi from escaping with it.<ref name="Followers">''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: The Followers]]''</ref> Sidious later executed the man for his failure.<ref name="END" />

The Sun Guard fought in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], where their [[2nd Regiment]] was crushed by [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Solomahal/Legends|Solomahal]].<ref name="RaT" /> In the early stages of the war, two Sun Guards were deployed during a [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]]-led [[Attack on Kashyyyk|attack]] on the Republic-held world of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. There, the pair confronted two Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] next to the [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400 light freighter]] ''[[Star Runner]]'', and the Guardsmen were defeated and killed.<ref name="Kinect Star Wars">''[[Kinect Star Wars]]''</ref> One Sun Guard mercenary named [[Sarsius Torne]] was recruited by the [[mutant]] [[Geneticist/Legends|genetic]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] [[Zeta Magnus]] to serve as the prime clone for a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Zeta Magnus's clone army|army]]. Torne trained the first four clones designated as [[Tark Squad]] who would infiltrate the Republic's [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]] disgused as [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]].<ref name="SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> During the conflict, rogue factions of the Sun Guard led a mercenary army in a campaign against the [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]] and carried out an [[Destruction of Emberlene|orbital bombardment]] of the Shadow Guard's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Emberlene]]. Near the end of the war,<ref name="GAW" /> when Sidious' plans for the Sun Guard were at last fulfilled, his apprentice [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] ordered his own minion [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] to execute most of the organization. Several of the more devoted Sun Guards, were placed in Palpatine's [[Red Guard]],<ref name="END" /> later to join his [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]]. Those who were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] were selected as candidates for the [[Emperor's Shadow Guard]], while others like [[Kenix Jir]] became [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] templates.<ref name="GAW" />

Palpatine's rise to power and manipulations of the Sun Guard split Thyrsian society, with most Thyrsians supporting the Emperor and viewing the Royal Guard as the Sun Guard's successors. Many Thyrsians enlisted in the ranks of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] and the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], but some saw Palpatine's manipulations as detrimental to the Sun Guards and sought to restore their traditions. Thyrsus became an isolationist world after Palpatine's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], but a new generation of Sun Guards appeared during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] of [[25 ABY/Legends|25]]–[[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. Those Sun Guards defended Thyrsus against the extragalactic [[Yuuzhan Vong]] and sought to restore their organization's traditions, reputation, and honor.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

==Equipment and techniques==
[[File:Moxin_Tark.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Bounty hunter Moxin Tark, wearing Sun Guard replica armor.]]
Members of the Sun Guard wore helmets similar to those of the Republic's [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]],<ref name="END" /> which were likely inspired by them.<ref name="SAV">[[Scum and Villainy (sourcebook)|''Scum and Villainy'']]</ref> The visor of the Sun Guard helmet could image thermal data, as well as [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] and [[Ultraviolet/Legends|ultraviolet]] wavelengths. A [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinocular]] scope in the helmet aided in surveillance and sniping. The Guard covered themselves with heavy, characteristically black armor, although other color variants such as yellow,<ref name="GAW" /><ref name="END" /> silver,<ref name="Darth Plagueis" /> and red existed.<ref name="Kinect Star Wars" /> While resistant to damage, Sun Guard armor did little to restrict movement.<ref name="GAW" /> Sun Guards also donned belt-spats around their waists, which later inspired the [[Kama/Legends|kama]] worn by the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="GGAR">{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}}</ref>

Worn by one of the most feared military organizations in history, the Sun Guard uniform was instantly recognizable and widely considered terrifying.<ref name="GAW" /> Along with the uniform of the Mandalorian [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], it was the inspiration for the Galactic Empire in designing the armor of its feared [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guard]].<ref name="ISB" /> Before his death, the Sun Guard who failed to retrieve Adas's holocron fathered a Royal Guard of his own, [[Carnor Jax]]; when Jax took up a leadership role in the Empire, he donned a black guardsman's uniform inspired by the coloration of his father's Sun Guard armor.<ref name="END" /> The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Moxin Tark]] was one of several hunters to obtain Sun Guard armor and weapons,<ref name="GAW" /> and he outfitted himself in a replica version of their armor.<ref name="WBC" /> [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Nashiak Llalik]], an Imperial armor designer, found inspiration in the armor of the Sun Guard, spurring him when he grew up to design the suits of armor used by many of the Empire's specialized [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] units.<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> [[Luskin Exovar]], a onetime [[Scout/Legends|scout]], owned a pair of scarred Sun Guard battle helmets, part of his private collection amassed during his travels.<ref name="WHOSAV">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]''</ref>

Though Sith cultists, the Sun Guard generally lacked Force-sensitivity<ref name="END" />—although exceptions existed.<ref name="GAW" /> They were trained in stealth and melee combat,<ref name="TPMvgm">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]] manual</ref> and preferred to face their opponents at close range.<ref name="GAW" /> The [[Echani (martial art)/Legends|echani]] [[Unarmed combat/Legends|martial art]] was thought to have originated with their techniques, modified by Palpatine's experts to be more brutal and deadly.<ref name="HG">''[[Hero's Guide]]''</ref> Sun Guard armor was designed to aid in up-close combat; [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]] in the [[Gauntlet/Legends|gauntlets]] and spikes in the boots and knee pads were common, and the forearm gauntlets and fist plates could be quickly heated to temperatures capable of burning flesh. Many Sun Guards augmented their array of offensive capabilities with [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]] and [[Dart shooter|dart launchers]] under their [[Limb/Legends|arms]].<ref name="GAW" />

The common weapon of the Sun Guard was the [[Pike/Legends|pike]], which they held in near-sacred reverence. Though this weapon had many variants, most were about a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] in length, wielded two-handed, and equipped with blades on either end. These blades tended to be double-edged vibroblades, often with [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] ore in them to resist [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]].<ref name="GAW" /> Ritual pikes known as [[solar pike]]s,<ref name="Author's Cut" /> which the Sun Guard inherited from the Echani, could be activated by a control in the grip to heat the tips to red-hot temperatures. [[Force pike/Legends|Force pikes]] had [[Stun/Legends|stun]] modules in either end to shock the wielder's enemies into paralysis. In their battles against the Mandalorians, the Sun Guard developed ways to use pikes as anti-air weapons, disabling or overloading the jetpacks that their opponents relied on and they held in contempt.<ref name="GAW" /> The Sun Guard used other weapons as well; at least one Guard armed himself with a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]].<ref name="TPMvg" />

Sun Guard were almost unmatched in combat skills; only Mandalorians could stand against them. Like the Echani, they could read body language to predict the moves of their opponents. They could match any opponent in personal combat, and those who fought in [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] pits sometimes got extra credits for their skills. Sun Guards had skill in urban warfare as well, trained in tactics of sweeping buildings and eliminating enemies block by block.<ref name="GAW" /> Some Sun Guards participated in space combat too; for his mission to retrieve Adas' holocron, one of the Sun Guards piloted a sleek gray ship, in which he attempted to fight off a Jedi vessel.<ref name="Followers" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Sun Guards? Drop-dead scariest fighters I ever saw. Fools should have remembered one thing, though - never trust a Sith.|A holorecording of Solomahal (cut from ''Galaxy at War'')|<ref name="blog" />}}
The Sun Guard were created as a brief mention in the first edition of the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' by [[Greg Gorden]]. There it was stated that they provided the inspiration for the design of Imperial Royal Guard armor, a fact that would later be used in illustrating Moxin Tark—and his Sun Guard lookalike armor—for [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]. Most of the Sun Guard's appearances in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity were these sorts of brief mentions, until [[Abel G. Peña]] greatly expanded on their history in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]''. In addition to tying the Sun Guard into various existing aspects of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]]—including [[Carnor Jax]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]'' comic series—''Evil Never Dies'' [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the group into two previous works. The [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]] included a [[Coruscant mercenary|mercenary]] [[Thug leader|boss]] dressed like a black-colored Royal Guard, which ''Evil Never Dies'' made the first true appearance of a Sun Guard.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24245850|board=Literature|thread=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties|user=Halagad_Ventor|date=2006-06-28|time=2:50 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20151001233422/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/evil-never-dies-the-sith-dynasties.24202765/page-8}}</ref> ''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: The Followers]]'', meanwhile, included a scene where a mysterious servant of the Sith kills the antagonist Norval; this was revealed in ''Evil Never Dies'' to be a Sun Guard as well.

Working alongside Peña, [[Daniel Wallace]] significantly expanded further on the Sun Guard in the [[2009]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy at War]]''. Having just written the entry on [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]], and seeing a connection with the Sun Guards' focus on armor, Wallace decided to make the Sun Guards and Mandalorians enemies. He also built upon the Echani connection mentioned in earlier sources, creating a "male-dominant, hot, and angry" group to contrast with the Echani's "female-dominant, cold, and controlled" nature. For the ranks of the Sun Guard, Wallace used names reminiscent of the [[Wikipedia:Roman Empire|Roman Empire]]. Among his references to earlier works, Wallace called upon a line from the second draft of [[George Lucas]]' script for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'']], which told a prophecy of a savior known as "the son of the suns". Though a previous reference to the cut line in another work of Wallace's had been removed by [[Lucasfilm]], the one in ''Galaxy at War'' passed editing, much to his surprise.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|danwallace|150|Endnotes from Galaxy at War}}</ref>

In [[2012]], the Sun Guards featured in the novel [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']], by [[James Luceno]], which elaborated upon the Guards' relationship with the Sith. Later that year, the Guards received a further appearance in the game ''[[Kinect Star Wars]]'', in which two Sun Guards were adversaries faced by the player in the game's "Jedi Destiny" mode. A Sun Guard also featured as a challengeable opponent in ''Kinect''{{'s}} "Duel of the Fates" mini-game. [[Jason Fry]], author of the 2012 reference guide ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', originally intended to include a "unit profile" on the Sun Guards in the book, as they served his purposes of jumping around the galactic timeline and mixing familiar elements with more obscure continuity. The organization had already been covered in ''Galaxy at War'', so the section was ultimately cut for space. On [[November 4]], [[2013]], the second entry of the ''[[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut]]'' blog series included the cut section discussing the Sun Guard, introducing those elements into canon.
